records.ety: [Cf. rimple, and D. rimpelen to wrinkle, rompelig rough, uneven, G. rümpfen to wrinkle, MHG. rümphen, OHG. rimpfan, Gr. "ra`mfos the crooked beak of birds of prey, <?/ to roam.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rumpled p. pr. & vb. n. Rumpling (<?/).]

defs: To make uneven; to form into irregular inequalities; to wrinkle; to crumple; as, to rumple an apron or a cravat.
quotes: They would not give a dog's ear of their most rumpled and ragged Scotch paper for twenty of your fairest assignats.

defs: A fold or plait; a wrinkle.
